cards.front: Backbone area
cards.back: Area 0 — all other areas must connect to it; carries inter-area traffic; cannot be made stub or NSSA

cards.front: Area 0 — all other areas must connect to it; carries inter-area traffic; cannot be made stub or NSSA
Standard area
Default; accepts all LSA types (1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
Stub area
cards.back: Blocks Type 4 and Type 5 (external) LSAs; ABR injects default route; useful for spoke areas with no external routes needed

cards.front: Blocks Type 4 and Type 5 (external) LSAs; ABR injects default route; useful for spoke areas with no external routes needed
Totally stubby area
cards.back: Cisco proprietary; blocks Type 3, 4, 5; ONLY default route propagated in; smallest LSDB

cards.front: Cisco proprietary; blocks Type 3, 4, 5; ONLY default route propagated in; smallest LSDB
NSSA
cards.back: Not-So-Stubby Area — like stub but allows a local ASBR to inject external routes as Type 7 LSAs; ABR converts Type 7 → Type 5 outbound

cards.front: Not-So-Stubby Area — like stub but allows a local ASBR to inject external routes as Type 7 LSAs; ABR converts Type 7 → Type 5 outbound
Totally NSSA
cards.back: Cisco proprietary; NSSA + blocks Type 3 (default only) + allows local ASBR Type 7

cards.front: Cisco proprietary; NSSA + blocks Type 3 (default only) + allows local ASBR Type 7
Which areas block Type 5 (external) LSAs?
cards.back: Stub, totally stubby, NSSA, totally NSSA. All except standard.

cards.front: Which area type allows external routes to be injected from within itself?
cards.back: NSSA (and totally NSSA) — via Type 7 LSAs from a local ASBR.

cards.front: Which areas receive only a default route (no Type 3 summaries)?

Stub command (under `router ospf`): [...]
Totally stubby: area X stub no-summary (on ABR only)
NSSA: area X nssa
Totally NSSA: area X nssa no-summary (on ABR only)

Stub command (under `router ospf`): [...]
Totally stubby: area X stub no-summary (on ABR only)
NSSA: area X nssa
Totally NSSA: area X nssa no-summary (on ABR only)

Stub command (under `router ospf`): [...]
Totally stubby: area X stub no-summary (on ABR only)
NSSA: area X nssa
Totally NSSA: area X nssa no-summary (on ABR only)

Stub command (under `router ospf`): [...]
Totally stubby: area X stub no-summary (on ABR only)
NSSA: area X nssa
Totally NSSA: area X nssa no-summary (on ABR only)
Can Area 0 be configured as stub or NSSA?
cards.back: No — backbone area must accept all LSA types.

cards.front: Two routers in a stub area won't form adjacency if their [...] (E-bit in Hello) doesn't match.
![[area-types-filter.svg]]
cards.back: Area types as LSA filters: Standard passes all. Stub blocks Type 4/5. Totally Stubby blocks 3/4/5 (default only). NSSA blocks 4/5 but allows local Type 7. Totally NSSA blocks 3/4/5, allows local Type 7.
